assignment06

Author

Xiaohan Xie 

suppressPackageStartupMessages({
  library(AnnotationHub)
  library(GenomicRanges)
  library(ggplot2)
  #library(memes) # for the meme-based methods(still need to install MEME) -- COMMENT OUT when using alternatives 
  library(motifmatchr) # for scanning sequences for matches of given motifs
  library(Biostrings) # for handling sequences
  library(MotifDb) # database of motifs
  library(TFBSTools) # for handling some motif formats
  library(universalmotif) # for converting motifs to various formats
  library(PWMEnrich) # for R-based motif enrichment analysis
  library(GenomeInfoDb)
  library(ensembldb)
  library(rtracklayer) 
  library(dplyr)
})
Warning: package 'dbplyr' was built under R version 4.5.2
Warning: package 'ggplot2' was built under R version 4.5.2
See system.file("LICENSE", package="MotifDb") for use restrictions.
Warning: package 'dplyr' was built under R version 4.5.2

Obtaining peak sequences

Transcription factor chosen: EGR1 Biosample chosen: Homo sapiens liver tissue female child (4 years)

download.file("https://www.encodeproject.org/files/ENCFF768POQ/@@download/ENCFF768POQ.bed.gz", "hsLiver_EGR1_ENCFF768POQ.bed.gz")
peaks <- rtracklayer::import("hsLiver_EGR1_ENCFF768POQ.bed.gz", format="NarrowPeak")
seqlevelsStyle(peaks) <- "Ensembl" 
head(peaks)
GRanges object with 6 ranges and 6 metadata columns:
      seqnames              ranges strand |        name     score signalValue
         <Rle>           <IRanges>  <Rle> | <character> <numeric>   <numeric>

  [3]        5 151447398-151447734      * |        <NA>      1000     395.214
  [4]       10 102714282-102714658      * |        <NA>      1000     385.534
  [5]       17   49788988-49789354      * |        <NA>      1000     370.839
  [6]       16   31179823-31180242      * |        <NA>      1000     342.784
         pValue    qValue      peak
      <numeric> <numeric> <integer>
  [1]        -1   3.74842       241
  [2]        -1   3.74842       209
  [3]        -1   3.74842       174
  [4]        -1   3.74842       185

Get the corresponding GRCh38 genome:

ah <- AnnotationHub()
query_results <- AnnotationHub::query(ah, c("Homo Sapiens", "TwoBit", "GRCh38"))
query_df <- data.frame(query_results$ah_id, query_results$rdatadateadded) 
query_df %>% arrange(desc(query_results.rdatadateadded))
    query_results.ah_id query_results.rdatadateadded

genome <- ah[["AH82402"]]
loading from cache
genome_seqs <- import(genome)
peak_seqs <- Biostrings::getSeq(genome, peaks)

Identify instances of the factor’s motif

Get the desired motif

# we search for "EGR1" in the motif database
motifs <- query(MotifDb, c("EGR1"))
# there are several matching motifs:
names(motifs)
 [1] "Hsapiens-HOCOMOCOv13-EGR1.H13CORE.0.PS.A"            
 [2] "Hsapiens-HOCOMOCOv10-EGR1_HUMAN.H10MO.A"             
 [3] "Hsapiens-HOCOMOCOv10-EGR1_HUMAN.H10MO.S"             
 [4] "Mmusculus-HOCOMOCOv10-EGR1_MOUSE.H10MO.A"            
 [5] "Hsapiens-HOCOMOCOv11-core-A-EGR1_HUMAN.H11MO.0.A"    
 [6] "NA-HOMER-Egr1(Zf)/K562-Egr1-ChIP-Seq(GSE32465)/Homer"
 [7] "Mmusculus-JASPAR_CORE-Egr1-MA0162.1"                 
 [8] "Hsapiens-JASPAR_2014-EGR1-MA0162.2"                  
 [9] "Mmusculus-jaspar2016-Egr1-MA0162.1"                  
[10] "Hsapiens-jaspar2016-EGR1-MA0162.2"                   
[11] "Mmusculus-jaspar2018-Egr1-MA0162.1"                  
[12] "Hsapiens-jaspar2018-EGR1-MA0162.2"                   
[13] "Hsapiens-jaspar2018-EGR1-MA0162.3"                   
[14] "Hsapiens-jaspar2022-EGR1-MA0162.4"                   
[15] "Hsapiens-jaspar2024-EGR1-MA0162.5"                   
[16] "Hsapiens-jolma2013-EGR1"                             
[17] "Hsapiens-jolma2013-EGR1-2"                           
[18] "Mmusculus-jolma2013-Egr1"                            
[19] "Hsapiens-SwissRegulon-EGR1.SwissRegulon"             
[20] "Mmusculus-UniPROBE-Egr1.UP00007"                     
# we select one:
motif <- motifs[["Hsapiens-HOCOMOCOv10-EGR1_HUMAN.H10MO.S"]]
motif
          1          2          3           4           5          6
A 0.1515633 0.04398516 0.05988341 0.003709592 0.009538951 0.07578166
C 0.1886592 0.06041335 0.82829889 0.001059883 0.013248543 0.01907790
G 0.3184950 0.88288288 0.01854796 0.993640700 0.490726020 0.90196078
T 0.3412825 0.01271860 0.09326974 0.001589825 0.486486486 0.00317965
            7          8           9          10         11
A 0.025437202 0.01218866 0.089030207 0.021727610 0.09062003
C 0.065712772 0.01006889 0.748277689 0.007419184 0.06518283
G 0.900900901 0.97774245 0.007419184 0.955484897 0.60943296
T 0.007949126 0.00000000 0.155272920 0.015368309 0.23476418
# we visualize it:
#view_motifs(motifs[1:2])
view_motifs(motif)

Using motifmatchr:

# convert the sequences to fasta:
Biostrings::writeXStringSet(genome_seqs, "genome_seqs.fa")
motif2 <- convert_motifs(motif, class="TFBSTools-PWMatrix")
Note: motif [motif] has an empty nsites slot, using 100.
motif_hits <- motifmatchr::matchMotifs(motif2, subject=peaks, genome=Rsamtools::FaFile("genome_seqs.fa"),
                                out="positions")
# the higher the score the better
motif_hits <- motif_hits[[1]] # we scanned for just one motif, so we get the results for that motif
head(motif_hits)
GRanges object with 6 ranges and 1 metadata column:
      seqnames              ranges strand |     score
         <Rle>           <IRanges>  <Rle> | <numeric>
  [1]       17   51153508-51153518      + |   14.0758
  [2]        3   42600568-42600578      - |   15.7005
  [3]        5 151447578-151447588      + |   18.4735
  [4]       10 102714450-102714460      - |   18.2636
  [5]       17   49789165-49789175      + |   18.5603
  [6]       16   31180091-31180101      + |   14.5171
  -------
  seqinfo: 23 sequences from an unspecified genome; no seqlengths
hist(motif_hits$score)

Of all the peaks, what proportion contains a motif for the factor?

overlaps <- overlapsAny(peaks,motif_hits) # cannot use within here as we need the proportion of peaks having overlaps
counts_contain <- sum(overlaps)
proportion_contain <- mean(overlaps)
sprintf("Of the %d peaks, %d (%.1f%%) contain a motif.", 
        length(peaks), counts_contain, proportion_contain*100)
[1] "Of the 8422 peaks, 2573 (30.6%) contain a motif."

Of all instances of that motif in the genome, what proportion is bound by the factor (i.e.has a peak)?

motif_across_genome <- matchMotifs(motif2, subject=genome_seqs, out="positions")[[1]]
names(motif_across_genome) <- names(genome_seqs)
motif_across_genome <- as(motif_across_genome, "GRanges")
head(motif_across_genome)
GRanges object with 6 ranges and 0 metadata columns:
      seqnames      ranges strand
         <Rle>   <IRanges>  <Rle>
  [1]        1 10819-10829      *
  [2]        1 10895-10905      *
  [3]        1 10971-10981      *
  [4]        1 11019-11029      *
  [5]        1 12741-12751      *
  [6]        1 16723-16733      *
  -------
  seqinfo: 194 sequences from an unspecified genome; no seqlengths
overlaps <- overlapsAny(motif_across_genome, peaks) # could use within to be stricter ?
counts_contain <- sum(overlaps)
proportion_contain <- mean(overlaps)
sprintf("Of the %d motif instances, %d (%.1f%%) overlap a peak.", 
        length(motif_across_genome), counts_contain, proportion_contain*100)
[1] "Of the 531405 motif instances, 7945 (1.5%) overlap a peak."
